narrative:
José Raul Rangel is Mexico's goalkeeper, currently holding an overall rating of 7.35/10. This places him among the top 2% of goalkeepers participating in this tournament. His rating range of 6.94–7.77 reflects his performance across a significant match sample.

Evidencing his primary role, Rangel averages 1.552 saves per 90 minutes at this World Cup. He also contributes defensively with 0.776 clearances per 90 minutes. His expected assists per 90 minutes remains low at 0.002 in the World Cup and 0.007 at club level, indicating a traditional shot-stopping focus rather than playmaking from the back.

Rangel is a pivotal figure for Mexico, with 82% of his overall rating derived from consistent club form over the last two seasons. Scouts and fans should continue to observe his shot-stopping ability and commanding presence in the box. His contributions over the 348 minutes played in this World Cup underscore his importance to the squad.
